title: Kral Nevil
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=GFFA |image= |name=Kral Nevil |homeworld=[[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]] |birth= |death= |species=[[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]]''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children=[[Turl Nevil]]''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]'' |affiliation=*[[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Corps]] *[[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]] *[[Alliance-in-exile]] *[[Jedi Coalition]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|My son didn't die to put a sadistic despot in power. I look to you to ensure his life wasn't wasted.|Kral to [[Cha Niathal]] about his son [[Turl Nevil]]|''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''}} '''Kral Nevil''' was a male [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] member of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]. From [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]] to [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], he flew as [[Rogue Two/Legends|Rogue Two]] and [[Gavin Darklighter]]'s wingman. ==Biography== ===The Yuuzhan Vong War=== ====Reconnaissance and defense==== {{Quote|It looks reef-raked in high seas, Colonel. What could have done it?''"
"''I don't know, Two, but I don't think we'll like the answer when we get it.|Kral Nevil and Gavin Darklighter comment on the state of the ''Free Lance''|}} A [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Kral Nevil joined the [[New Republic Starfighter Corps/Legends|New Republic Starfighter Corps]] and eventually secured a place in [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], a legendary [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]]. Nevil had a [[Kral Nevil's wife|wife]] and a son, [[Turl Nevil|Turl]]. By [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], Nevil, who had attained the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]], was flying as [[Rogue Two/Legends|Rogue Two]] alongside [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Gavin Darklighter]]. Rogue Squadron had been assigned to the [[New Republic First Fleet|fleet]] of [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]], who wished to investigate reports of an alien incursion in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] in the early months of the year. Nevil, who had the nickname and [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] "Deuce", was stationed with his fellow [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] were stationed on Kre'fey's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Bothan Assault Cruiser]] ''[[Ralroost]]''. Upon reports of [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] activity in an Outer Rim [[Star system/Legends|star system]], Darklighter led the Rogues out into [[Realspace/Legends|space]] to reconnoiter the situation. [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Inyri Forge]] soon detected unknown signatures in the system's [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]], which were soon confirmed to be [[Ugly/Legends|Uglies]], starfighters cobbled together out of various parts. As Nevil and his fellow pilots engaged the Uglies, it soon became clear that the starships were already heavily damaged. Darklighter made contact with an [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] which served as the pirates' base of operations and called for [[Urias Xhaxin]], the captain of the vessel, to order his forces to stand down. As Darklighter negotiated with Xhaxin, Nevil protected his wingmate from a [[TYE-wing]]. Xhaxin subsequently surrendered and Nevil observed that significant damage had been incurred by [[Free Lance|the Nebulon-B escort frigate]] in an engagement against an unknown foe. Making use of Xhaxin's information, Kre'fey set up a reconnaissance mission to another star system where the pirate captain claimed his forces had been ambushed by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], an alien [[Species/Legends|species]] reported to have launched an [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. Rogue Squadron flew cover for a [[T-65BR X-wing reconnaissance starfighter|T-65R reconnaissance X-wing]] as it scouted the system. Nevil, Darklighter, and the rest of One flight trailed behind the recon starfighter while Majors Inyri Forge and [[Alinn Varth]] fanned out to cover a wider area. Soon, six [[Yorik-et|coralskippers]], as the organic Yuuzhan Vong starfighters had been termed, emerged and engaged Rogue Squadron. Against the asteroid-like starfighters, which utilized [[dovin basal]]s to create miniature [[Black hole/Legends|black holes]] to strip [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] off [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] starfighters and to suck in incoming [[Laser/Legends|laser]] beams, Nevil and his fellow pilots were forced to adopt new tactics. Nevil again aided Darklighter during the brief engagement, scoring several kills against the Yuuzhan Vong pilots while protecting his wingmate. Although Nevil and Darklighter were able to save the life of [[Rogue Seven/Legends|Rogue Seven]], the [[Krish (species)/Legends|Krish]] [[Ligg Panat]], Major Alinn Varth reported following the end of the skirmish that two pilots from her flight, [[Dinger]] and [[Tik (Rogue Squadron)|Tik]], had perished. Rogue Squadron remained in the system until Admiral Kre'fey arrived with the ''Ralroost''. Following the minor engagement, Nevil and Rogue Squadron accompanied Kre'fey to [[Dubrillion/Legends|Dubrillion]], where the Yuuzhan Vong were threatening the [[Planet/Legends|world]] as it was evacuated by a convoy of starships from nearby [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]]. Upon the arrival of the Agamarian convoy, the Yuuzhan Vong forces lurking in the [[Dubrillion system/Legends|system's]] [[Lando's Folly|asteroid belt]] had sent half a dozen coralskippers to engage the relief vessels. Nevil and his fellow pilots fought off the Yuuzhan Vong, who over the next four days settled into a pattern of testing Roque Squadron's response time with minor incursions. When the time came for the Agamarian convoy to leave the Dubrillion system, Darklighter led Nevil and the other Rogue Squadron pilots out to form a small cordon between the departing starships and the Yuuzhan Vong. In the subsequent [[Evacuation of Dubrillion|engagement]], Rogue Squadron took the fight to a Yuuzhan Vong [[miid ro'ik]] [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] and inflicted damage upon the [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]. Eventually, however, [[Rock-One|the cruiser]] powered up its dovin basals and created an artificial gravity well which forced the Agamarian convoy to divert to [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]]. Nevil and his fellow pilots traveled to Dantooine in order to protect the convoy against further Yuuzhan Vong attacks. ====Dantooine and Ithor==== Kral participated in key engagements against the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] at [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], [[Ithor/Legends|Ithor]], and [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]]. ===Second Galactic Civil War=== By [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], Kral had transferred from [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] operations to the naval forces. He became captain of the [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']] and served as the chief aide of [[Darth Caedus]] aboard the command ship following the [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|Battle of Kashyyyk]]. Kral's son [[Turl Nevil|Turl]] was killed when the Corellians fired [[Centerpoint Station]] on their own fleet in hopes of killing Caedus, something that greatly affected Kral. Caedus tried to express his sympathies to him. Later, when Kral tried to intervene and keep Caedus from killing [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Patra Tebut]] after a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] led team was able to board and retrieve the [[Hapan]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Allana Djo Solo|Allana]], Caedus responded by throwing Kral against a bulkhead. After killing Tebut, Caedus was unable to determine if Kral's expression was one of fear, anger, or acceptance. Caedus decided that he no longer liked either the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] or the [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]]. After Darth Caedus murdered Lieutenant Tebut, Nevil met with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Cha Niathal]] regarding the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord's]] actions. Niathal was just as appalled over what had happened, and while she very much wanted to have Solo arrested and charged with murder, she knew that the [[Galactic Alliance Guard]] would not permit Solo to either be arrested or charged. Nevil remained as Captain of the ''Anakin Solo'' until the [[Second Battle of Fondor]]. When Solo refused an order to cease bombardment of [[Fondor/Legends|Fondor]], Niathal attempted to relieve him of his command, but most of the fleet remained loyal to Solo. Nevil then used an [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] to leave the ''Anakin Solo'', and [[Desertion|defected]] to the [[Alliance-in-exile|faction]] of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] loyal to Admiral Niathal. ==Personality and traits== Nevil was a capable starfighter pilot, who used Quarren idioms and was inquisitive. Nevil also had a good-natured relationship with Darklighter, his squadron leader. ==Behind the scenes== Kral Nevil was confirmed to have fought during the First Battle of Coruscant during the Yuuzhan Vong War in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. ==Appearances== *''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught]]'' {{1st}} *[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide I: Onslaught (abridged
